Class RequestRegistry

java.lang.Object
com.habbashx.request.registry.RequestRegistry
public class RequestRegistry extends Object
Registry that maps each HttpMethod to its BuildStrategy and dispatches HTTP requests both synchronously and asynchronously.

On construction, the four built-in strategies (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) are registered from RequestMethod. HttpExecutor calls this class to send requests without needing to know which strategy is used for which verb.

Uses an EnumMap for O(1) strategy lookup with minimal memory overhead.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    RequestRegistry(HttpClient client)
    Creates a registry and registers the four default HTTP method strategies.

    Description
    String
    execute(HttpMethod method, String url, String body, String[] headers)
    Sends an HTTP request synchronously and returns the response body as a string.
    CompletableFuture<String>
    executeAsync(HttpMethod method, String url, String body, String[] headers)
    Sends an HTTP request asynchronously and returns a future resolving to the response body.

  • Constructor Details

    • RequestRegistry

      public RequestRegistry(HttpClient client)
      Creates a registry and registers the four default HTTP method strategies.
      Parameters:
      client - the HttpClient to use for all dispatched requests

  • Method Details

    • execute

      public String execute(HttpMethod method, String url, String body, String[] headers) throws Exception
      Sends an HTTP request synchronously and returns the response body as a string.
      Parameters:
      method - the HTTP method to use
      url - the fully-resolved request URL
      body - the request body; may be empty for GET/DELETE
      headers - alternating header name/value pairs
      Returns:
      the raw HTTP response body string
      Throws:
      Exception - if the request fails at the transport layer
      IllegalArgumentException - if no strategy is registered for method

    • executeAsync

      public CompletableFuture<String> executeAsync(HttpMethod method, String url, String body, String[] headers)
      Sends an HTTP request asynchronously and returns a future resolving to the response body.
      Parameters:
      method - the HTTP method to use
      url - the fully-resolved request URL
      body - the request body; may be empty for GET/DELETE
      headers - alternating header name/value pairs
      Returns:
      a CompletableFuture that completes with the raw response body string
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if no strategy is registered for method
